# Janki

> Personal Windows desktop app for learning Japanese with FSRS-6 SRS and WaniKani-style kanji progression.

## Stack

- Tauri 2.x (desktop shell, Rust backend)
- Svelte 5 + Vite (frontend SPA)
- shadcn-svelte + Tailwind CSS 4 (UI)
- SQLite via tauri-plugin-sql (local database)
- FSRS-6 via ts-fsrs (spaced repetition)
- anki-reader (Anki .apkg import)

## Key Files

- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Project rules and conventions
- [README.md](README.md): Architecture, prerequisites, data sources
- [tasks/plan-repo.md](tasks/plan-repo.md): Full development plan (4 phases)
- [.claude/references/infrastructure.md](.claude/references/infrastructure.md): Desktop app architecture
- [.claude/references/design-guardrails.md](.claude/references/design-guardrails.md): UI rules, colors, typography, accessibility
- [.claude/references/tools.md](.claude/references/tools.md): CLI tools reference
- [.claude/agent-memory/decisions.md](.claude/agent-memory/decisions.md): Architectural decisions and rationale

## Architecture

```
Tauri Window
  └─ Svelte 5 Frontend (WebView2)
       ├─ Views: Dashboard, Review, KanjiMap, Decks, Stats, Search, Settings
       ├─ SRS: FSRS-6 (deck cards) + WaniKani fixed-interval (kanji progression)
       └─ Data: SQLite ($APPDATA/janki/janki.db)
```

## Development Phases

1. Foundation -- scaffold, layout, theme, SQLite schema
2. Core -- SRS engine, Anki import, flashcard review, kanji progression
3. Polish -- TTS, search, statistics, keyboard shortcuts
4. Ship -- auto-updater, system tray, backup, grammar, reading practice
